meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Founders: Analytics Setup to Finally Ship Your SaaS Project
content.seo_description: Stop stalling. Learn how to implement data-driven analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate your MVP and finally finish your SaaS project.
content.answer_block: You aren't failing to finish because of code; you're failing because of a lack of feedback loops. By integrating automated analytics, you transform guesswork into data-backed product decisions. This setup forces you to track user behavior, validate your value proposition, and maintain momentum by focusing strictly on high-conversion features.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is littered with 'perfect' codebases that never saw a single paying user. You feel the stagnation—the endless refactoring, the feature creep, and the gnawing uncertainty that prevents you from pushing to production. The problem isn't your technical skill; it's the lack of a compass to guide your build process.

True velocity comes from shipping, not perfecting. By instrumenting your MVP with surgical precision, you stop building for your ego and start building for your market. We will use Antigravity and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, giving you the visibility required to iterate rapidly and finally ship your product.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Identify the one action that signals value. For most SaaS, this is user signup or primary feature usage. We must define this before writing code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest the optimal tracking points for a North Star metric focused on user retention and conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Review my routes and identify where users drop off; propose a tracking schema for PostHog or Mixpanel.'

steps.title: Automate Event Instrumentation
steps.description: Stop manually wiring events. Use AI-assisted code injection to ensure every button click and form submission is tracked automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a wrapper component for all primary CTA buttons that automatically fires an analytics event with metadata on click.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Wrap all primary buttons in src/components/ui/button.tsx with an analytics tracking hook.'

steps.title: Implement User Identity Resolution
steps.description: Anonymous data is useless. Connect your database user ID to your analytics events to track the full lifecycle of a paying user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure middleware that attaches the current user's session ID to all analytics payloads without exposing PII.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Implement identity middleware in src/middleware.ts to sync user context with analytics provider.'

steps.title: Visualizing the Funnel
steps.description: Configure your dashboard to show the journey from landing page to paid subscription. This identifies the exact bottleneck killing your project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard configuration file that maps my current event stream to a standardized conversion funnel report.

steps.title: Automated Alerting for Stagnation
steps.description: Set up alerts so that if your primary metric drops, you are notified immediately, preventing the 'build-in-the-dark' trap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a cron job script that checks daily analytics data and sends a Slack alert if the conversion rate drops below 2%.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Add a monitor script in scripts/check-metrics.ts to alert on conversion dips.'

pro_tips: Track 'failed' events as aggressively as successful ones; knowing where users get frustrated is more valuable than knowing where they succeed.
Never over-instrument; track only the top 5 events that correlate with revenue to avoid 'analysis paralysis'.
Use feature flags alongside your analytics to test UI changes on 10% of users before a full rollout.
Automate your report generation to hit your inbox every Monday; if you don't look at the data, the data doesn't exist.
Keep your instrumentation code decoupled from business logic; use an abstraction layer so you can swap analytics providers in minutes.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, commit to a 7-day sprint where you only improve the metrics identified by your funnel report. Do not add new features until your conversion rate increases by 10%.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the technical architecture to stop building in the dark and start building a business. Use these tools to prove your value, secure your first customers, and ship your MVP with absolute confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
